sn5.9:1.2: Then, early in the morning, Selā the nun adjusted her robes and, taking her bowl & outer robe, went into Sāvatthī for alms. When she had gone for alms in Sāvatthī and had returned from her alms round, after her meal she went to the Grove of the Blind to spend the day.
sn5.9:1.3: Having gone deep into the Grove of the Blind, she sat down at the foot of a tree for the day’s abiding.
sn5.9:1.4: Then Māra the Evil One, wanting to arouse fear, horripilation, & terror in her, wanting to make her fall away from concentration,
sn5.9:1.5: approached her & addressed her in verse:
sn5.9:3.1: Then the thought occurred to Selā the nun:
sn5.9:3.2: “Now who has recited this verse—a human being or a non-human one?”
sn5.9:3.4: “This is Māra the Evil One who has recited this verse wanting to arouse fear, horripilation, & terror in me, wanting to make me fall away from concentration.”
sn5.9:3.5: Then, having understood that “This is Māra the Evil One,” she replied to him in verses:
sn5.9:4.2: nor is this misery made by another.
sn5.9:4.3: In dependence on a cause it comes into play.
sn5.9:4.4: With the dissolution of the cause it ceases.
sn5.9:6.1: in the same way, these aggregates, properties,
sn5.9:6.3: in dependence on a cause come into play.
sn5.9:6.4: With the dissolution of the cause they cease.”
sn5.9:7.1: Then Māra the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “Selā the nun knows me”—vanished right there.
